manuscript_profile.title: Great Expectations
manuscript_profile.target_audience: Adult readers of literary bildungsroman, social-moral novel, classic coming-of-age, class/identity novel, and moral psychological fiction

txt: Literary Bildungsroman / Social-Moral Novel / Class/Identity Fiction
It should not be evaluated as a simple rise-and-fall social mobility plot; its real engine is shame, misrecognition, and moral education.
Retrospective first-person voice fuses adult irony with child perception
Author retains ownership of manuscript content

optional_public_strings: A powerful moral premise: an orphan's social ascent reveals the corruption of shame and the hidden nobility of loyalty, labor, and remorse.
A DREAM evaluator must detect moral psychology as plot architecture, not merely character description.
Serial-middle expansion occasionally slows the central shame/revelation engine.
source_only_strings: Great Expectations is a benchmark bildungsroman because its architecture converts class aspiration into moral revelation and repair.
Do not impose modern thriller pacing or romance-closure expectations.
